species.notes: Cultivated in most parts of the world. Dill.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 7 July 2017

species.citation: L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 264 ; Buwalda, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1949) 131.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 22 December 2025

species.citation: (L.) Urb. , Fl. Bras. (Martius) 11(1) (1879) 287 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 360; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 238; Buwalda, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1949) 117; Romeroso et al., Asian J. Biol. Life Sc. 10 (2021) 437; ≡ Hydrocotyle asiatica L. , Sp. Pl., 2nd Ed. (1753) 234.
species.distribution: Pantropic. ALABAT, BASILAN, CAMIGUIN DE BABUYANES, HOMONHON, LUZON: Abra (photos), Aurora (photos), Bataan, Benguet, Ifugao, La Union, Laguna, Mountain Province, NCR, Nueva Vizcaya, Rizal, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, Bukidnon, Davao del Sur (photos), MINDORO, NEGROS, PALAWAN, SIARGAO.
species.notes: All over Malesia. Sunny or slightly shaded, fertile, damp sites, along streambanks, also between stones of pathways and alongside walls. 1-2500m.

species.citation: L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 256 ; Buwalda, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1949) 127.
species.notes: Indigenous from Mediterranean region to C Asia; cultivated in nearly all parts of the world. Coriander.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 7 July 2017

species.citation: (Pers.) Sprague ex Britton & P.Wilson , Sci. Surv. Porto Rico & Virgin Islands 6 (1925) 52 ; Sprague, J. Bot. 61 (1923) 131, nom. inval.; ≡ Apium leptophyllum (Pers.) F.Muell. ex Benth. , Fl. Austral. 3 (1866) 372 ; Buwalda, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 9 (1982) 569b; ≡ Pimpinella leptophylla Pers. , Syn. Pl. (Pers.) 1 (1805) 324. = Apium tenuifolium (Moench) Thell. , Ill. Fl. Mitt.-Eur. 5 (1926) 1140 ; Buwalda, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1949) 131; ≡ Cnidium tenuifolium Moench , Methodus (Moench) (1794) 98.
L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 242 ; Buwalda, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1949) 140.
species.distribution: Afghanistan, Africa, China, Europe, Middle East, Pakistan, W Asia.
species.notes: Spontaneous in Europe, N Africa, and temperate Asia, cultivated in all parts of the world. Carrot.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by P.B. Pelser, 23 August 2025

species.distribution: C & S America, Caribbean. MINDANAO: Cotabato.

species.citation: Mill. , Gard. Dict., 8th Ed. (1768) n.1 ; Gaertn., Fruct. 1 (1788) 105; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 359; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 239; Buwalda, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1949) 136.
species.distribution: Afghanistan, Africa, Europe, Middle East, Pakistan, W Asia.
species.notes: Indigenous in the Mediterranean region, cultivated in nearly all parts of the world.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by P.B. Pelser, 28 August 2023

species.citation: (Blume) DC. , Prodr. (A. P. de Candolle) 4 (1830) 138 ; Sp. Pl. (1753) 256; Buwalda, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1949) 136; ≡ Sium javanicum Blume , Bijdr. fl. Ned. Ind. (1826) 881. = Oenanthe laciniata Zoll. , Syst. Verz. (Zollinger) 2 (1854) 139 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 239.
species.distribution: Australia, Bangladesh, Borneo, Cambodia, China, E Asia, India, Japan, Java, Korea, Laos, Lesser Sunda Isls, Moluccas, Myanmar, Nepal, New Guinea, Pakistan, Ryukyu Isls, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Taiwan, Thailand, Vietnam. LUZON: Benguet, Ifugao, Laguna, Mountain Province, Nueva Vizcaya.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by P.B. Pelser, 30 August 2022

species.citation: (Mill.) Fuss , Fl. Transsilv. (1866) 254 ; Buwalda, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1949) 132. = Petroselinum vulgare Hill , Brit. Herb. (Hill) (1756) 424. = Carum petroselinum (L.) Benth. & Hook.f. , Gen. Pl. (Bentham & Hooker f.) 1 (1867) 891; ≡ Apium petroselinum L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 264.
Thunb. , Fl. Jap. (Thunberg) (1784) 117 ; Hatusima, Mem. Fac. Agr. Kagoshima Univ. 5 (1966) 42; Buwalda, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1949) 140.
species.distribution: Japan, Philippines, Ryukyu Isls, Taiwan. BATANES, ITBAYAT (photos).
species.notes: Crevices of cliffs along the seashore. 0m.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 10 October 2019

species.citation: Dalzell , Hooker's J. Bot. Kew Gard. Misc. 2 (1850) 261 ; Buwalda, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1949) 135. = Pimpinella pinetorum Merr. , PJS 29 (1926) 482.
species.notes: Along trails in pine forest, c. 2000m. Locally rather abundant.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 9 June 2026

species.citation: (Roxb.) Mousavi, Mozaff. & Zarre , Pl. Syst. Evol. 308 (2022) 2: 10; ≡ Carum involucratum (Roxb.) Baill. , Hist. Pl. (Baillon) 7 (1879) 179 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 239; ≡ Apium involucratum Roxb. , Asiat. Res. 11 (1810) 157. = Trachyspermum roxburghianum (DC.) Craib , Fl. Siam. 1 (1931) 788 ; Buwalda, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1949) 132; ≡ Ptychotis roxburghiana DC. , Prodr. (A. P. de Candolle) 4 (1830) 109.
species.notes: Of unknown provenance, now cultivated and subspontaneous in tropical SE Asia; throughout Malesia.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 23 February 2020

species.citation: Buch.-Ham. ex D.Don , Prodr. Fl. Nepal. (1825) 183. = Sanicula europaea var. javanica (Blume) H.Wolff , Pflanzenr. (Engler) IV (228) (1913) 64 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 238. ≠ Sanicula europaea auct. non L. ; Buwalda, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1949) 125.
species.distribution: Africa, Bhutan, Borneo, China, India, Java, Laos, Lesser Sunda Isls, Madagascar, Malay Peninsula, Moluccas, Myanmar, Nepal, Pakistan, Philippines, Sri Lanka, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Tibet, Vietnam. MINDANAO: Bukidnon, Cotabato (photos), Davao del Sur.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 30 August 2022

species.citation: (L.) Sprague , Bull. Misc. Inform. Kew 1929 (1929) 228 ; Buwalda, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1949) 132; ≡ Sison ammi L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 252. = Carum copticum (L.) Benth. & Hook.f. ex Hiern , Fl. Trop. Afr. (Oliver et al.) 3 (1877) 12 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 238; ≡ Ammi copticum L. , Mant. Pl. (1767) 56.
species.distribution: Afghanistan, Bangladesh, Middle East, Myanmar, Nepal, Pakistan, W Asia.
